zh.lifeSummary: 當Enoguen de Vitré, Dame de Châtillon-en-Vendelais於大約1060年出生在法國布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉維特雷時，她的父親Robert de Vitré I11058歲，母親Berthe DE CRAON18歲。她和Renaud I de Craon 2nd Lord of Craon至少有4個兒子。她去世於從1078年1月28日到1080年10月在法國迪瑟 德 內維斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Enoguen de Vitré, Dame de Châtillon-en-Vendelais于大约1060年出生在法国布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉維特雷时，她的父亲Robert de Vitré I11058岁，母亲Berthe DE CRAON18岁。她和Renaud I de Craon 2nd Lord of Craon至少有4个儿子。她去世于从1078年1月28日到1080年10月在法国迪瑟 德 內維斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vitré,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Vetro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Rocco, Angelo, Antonio, Armonda, Vincenzo.

nameMeanings.name: Dame
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: from Middle English, Old French dame ‘lady’ (from Latin domina ‘mistress’), applied as a nickname for a foppish young man whose outfit and manner have an effeminate elegance, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a lady. As a French name it may also be a nickname meaning ‘mother-in-law’.
English: possibly an East Anglian variant of   Dam   4.

variants.name: Dam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Hoa, Tuan, Ngoc, Thanh, Binh, Chanh, Duc, Hiep, Minh, Nu, Tien.  Southeast Asian Hong, Chan, Dong, Hon, Man, Oi, Ok, Phoung, Sang, Sokhom, Sun.
Vietnamese (Đàm): from the Chinese surnames 譚, 谈 and 覃, see Tan 1-3.
Cambodian: written ដាំ, possibly of Vietnamese origin (see above). The surname ដាំ also corresponds to the Khmer word meaning e.g. ‘to plant’.

en.lifeSummary: When Enoguen de Vitré, Dame de Châtillon-en-Vendelais was born about 1060, in Vitré, Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany, France, her father, Robert de Vitré I, was 11058 and her mother, Berthe DE CRAON, was 18. She had at least 4 sons with Renaud I de Craon 2nd Lord of Craon. She died from 28 January 1078 to October 1080, in Diocese of Nevers, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Enoguen de Vitré, Dame de Châtillon-en-Vendelais est née environ 1060, à Vitré, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, France, son père, Robert de Vitré I, avait 11058 ans et sa mère, Berthe DE CRAON, avait 18 ans. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils avec Renaud I de Craon 2nd Lord of Craon. Elle est décédée du 28 janvier 1078 au octobre 1080, à Diocèse de Nevers, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Enoguen de Vitré, Dame de Châtillon-en-Vendelais um 1060 in Vitré, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Vitré I 11058 und ihre Mutter Berthe DE CRAON 18. Sie hatte mit Renaud I de Craon 2nd Lord of Craon mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb von 28. Januar 1078 bis Oktober 1080 in Diocèse de Nevers, Frankreich.

Lei avuto almeno 4 figli con Renaud I de Craon 2nd Lord of Craon. Morì da 28 gennaio 1078 a ottobre 1080, nel Diocèse de Nevers, Francia.
